text:
Traffic congestion remains a critical urban challenge, contributing to significant fuel waste, economic productivity loss, and delays for emergency services. Traditional traffic management relies on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) using rigid round-robin scheduling, which fails to adapt to real-time fluctuations. While recent IoT-based solutions have attempted to use traffic density for control, they often struggle with accuracy—particularly regarding emergency vehicle detection—due to the scarcity of diverse, high-quality image datasets for training deep neural networks. 

This paper introduces a novel, multi-modal distributed IoT framework designed around two key practical observations. First, recognizing that road infrastructure changes infrequently, we propose a two-stage vehicle detector ("R-YOLO") that uses road masking to focus computational resources. This approach achieves 79.5% detection accuracy on the UA-DETRAC dataset while maintaining the low latency required for edge deployment. Second, to address the limitations of visual detection for priority vehicles, we implement an acoustic detection algorithm on an edge device to identify the unique siren signatures of emergency vehicles. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system detects emergency vehicles with an average accuracy of 99.4%, providing a high-precision, resilient solution for intelligent urban traffic management.
